location.office_time_required: 3 days per week (Tuesdays, Thursdays, and one flex day); up to 4 weeks remote per year

level_signals: 7+ years of experience in product at a high-growth tech startup or as a startup founder
Comp range $220K-$302.5K base, consistent with Staff IC level
Make decisions alongside company founders and the CEO

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have 7+ years of experience in product at a high-growth tech startup or as a startup founder.
You're an inspiring leader who is excited about the day-to-day of understanding our customers and working with a strong team to build improvements for them.
You're an extraordinarily fast learner. You have the natural curiosity and intellectual horsepower to deeply understand new topics and pick up new skills in a very short time.

You are relentlessly resourceful. When something needs to get done, you always find a way to make it happen. Even when the work is hard, you power through it and execute with limited information and ambiguity.
You are a creative problem-solver. You excel at identifying unintuitive solutions to tough problems.
You care deeply about developing products and solutions that will create the best experience for our customers.
You have a passion for optimizing processes. You get satisfaction from seeing how something is being done and finding ways to make the process faster, easier, and more efficient.

scope.surface: Brand-side of the Faire wholesale marketplace — workflows, programs, and products helping brands succeed on Faire
scope.business_model: B2B marketplace (wholesale platform connecting independent retailers and brands)
core_responsibilities: Deeply understand our customers, build strategies that anticipate and support their needs, and execute rapidly and with an eye to metrics impact to improve their experience
Build programs to support customers alongside shipping great technology products
Understand the business equation and flywheel that make our marketplace work – and accelerate it
Work with and learn from a broad range of world-class leaders across go-to-market, ops, and finance, as well as engineering, design, and data science
Make decisions alongside company founders and the CEO
Empower small businesses around the world to chase their dreams and invest in their local communities

green_flags: Compensation range explicitly stated and competitive ($220K-$302.5K base)
Direct collaboration with CEO and company founders signals high-impact role
Clear product surface (Brand-side of marketplace) with well-defined cross-functional partners
Well-known VC-backed company (Y Combinator, Sequoia, Lightspeed, etc.) with established product culture
Hybrid model with explicit in-office schedule and up to 4 weeks remote flexibility
red_flags: No mention of visa sponsorship — unclear for international candidates
Broad and somewhat generic qualifications language (relentlessly resourceful, creative problem-solver) makes differentiation criteria ambiguous
No specific domain experience (wholesale, retail, marketplace) listed as a requirement, but the role is highly marketplace-specific — implied depth may be expected
Pay range is very wide ($82.5K spread), making it difficult to assess likely offer level
Nice-to-haves section is entirely absent from JD — signals either high bar or underspecified role

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title 'Staff PM', comp range, and years requirement)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated in JD)
ambiguities: No explicit nice-to-haves section in JD; all qualifications are written as unconditional bullets under 'Qualifications', so all are treated as hard must-haves.

The founder/startup experience qualifier ('or as a startup founder') in the years requirement is unusual — it is unclear whether founding experience fully substitutes for product management experience or is additive.

Location context: SF pay range given explicitly, but the JD mentions offices in Toronto, London, New York, and Kitchener-Waterloo; it is unclear if this role is SF-only or could be based elsewhere — treated as SF primary given the pay range disclosure.
